steps.title: Convert the table to f(x) values
steps.content:
The table is for $y=f(x)+4$, but the question asks about the graph of $y=f(x)$. Subtract 4 from each listed $y$-value:
  • $f(21)=-8-4=-12$
  • $f(23)=8-4=4$
  • $f(25)=-8-4=-12$

steps.title: Use symmetry to write vertex form
steps.content: The equal values $f(21)=f(25)=-12$ show symmetry around $x=23$, and $f(23)=4$, so the vertex is $(23,4)$. Write $f(x)=a(x-23)^2+4$.
